text: I've never had a problem with drugs. I've had problems with the police.
speaker: Keith Richards
context: The Rolling Stones guitarist's widely-quoted quip about his run-ins with the law, reflecting his well-documented history of drug arrests in the 1960s-80s.

text: If you're gonna do it, you might as well fuckin' put your name above the door. It's always been about me anyway.
speaker: Liam Gallagher
context: Oasis frontman, on going solo and releasing music under his own name rather than a band name, in his trademark blunt interview style (Vice/Noisey interview).

text: I wouldn't have been surprised if the bloke from the lab had called back and said, 'Mr Osbourne, we appear to have found some blood in your alcohol.'
speaker: Ozzy Osbourne
context: Black Sabbath frontman, in his 2010 autobiography 'I Am Ozzy,' joking about needing a blood test before getting married in America.

text: I know it's supposed to be ladies first, but when it comes to chicken, there's no rules.
speaker: Snoop Dogg
context: During the fried-chicken cook-off on the premiere of VH1's 'Martha & Snoop's Potluck Dinner Party' (November 2016), where Snoop and Martha Stewart competed over who made the best fried chicken.

text: No matter how much money you have, no matter how big you are, how famous you are, they will still end up stealing your charger and your lighter.
speaker: Cardi B
context: Rapper, on her Instagram Live (October 2017), joking that even wealthy and famous friends will still walk off with your charger and lighter.

text: I have an opinion on everything, and if I don't have an opinion, I'll fucking make one up on the spot.
speaker: Noel Gallagher
context: Oasis songwriter/guitarist, on his reputation as an outspoken, gobshite interviewee, in one of his many candid press interviews.

text: It costs a lot of money to look this cheap.
speaker: Dolly Parton
context: Country music legend's signature self-deprecating quip about her flashy, glamorous image. A long-running "Dollyism" she has used in interviews and posted herself on her official Twitter/X account.

text: When you meet somebody for the first time, you're not meeting them. You're meeting their representative.
speaker: Chris Rock
context: Stand-up special 'Chris Rock: Bigger & Blacker' (HBO, 1999), during his bit on relationships

text: Why would you want to sleep in on a Sunday when you can go pay $18 for eggs?
speaker: Bill Burr
context: From Bill Burr's stand-up brunch bit (widely circulated; associated with his Netflix special 'Walk Your Way Out', 2017)

text: I hate McDonald's. I don't want to order my dinner by yelling into a clown's mouth.
speaker: Joan Rivers
context: From her 2012 book 'I Hate Everyone... Starting with Me'

text: Vegetarians, and their Hezbollah-like splinter faction, the vegans, are a persistent irritant to any chef worth a damn.
speaker: Anthony Bourdain
context: From Bourdain's 2000 memoir Kitchen Confidential, his blunt take on dietary restrictions in restaurant kitchens.

text: You play to win the game! Hello?! You play to win the game. You don't play it to just play it.
speaker: Herm Edwards
context: New York Jets head coach, press conference, October 30, 2002, responding to a question from NYT reporter Judy Battista about his 2-5 team's mindset

text: But they are who we thought they were! And we let 'em off the hook!
speaker: Dennis Green
context: Arizona Cardinals head coach, postgame press conference after a Monday Night Football loss to the Chicago Bears, October 16, 2006

text: Practice? We talking about practice. Not a game, not a game, not a game, we talking about practice.
speaker: Allen Iverson
context: Philadelphia 76ers guard, press conference, May 7, 2002, responding to questions about missing practices

text: I'm a man! I'm 40!
speaker: Mike Gundy
context: Oklahoma State head football coach, press conference, September 22, 2007, defending player Bobby Reid against a newspaper columnist

text: I'm just here so I won't get fined.
speaker: Marshawn Lynch
context: Seattle Seahawks running back at Super Bowl XLIX Media Day, January 2015, repeating the same line to nearly every reporter question to avoid NFL media-policy fines

text: It is what it is.
speaker: Bill Belichick
context: New England Patriots head coach, recurring deflecting answer used across many press conferences

text: My gran could do better, and she's dead!
speaker: Gordon Ramsay
context: One of Gordon Ramsay's signature kitchen put-downs on his cooking shows

text: You've baked a really lovely cake, but then you've used dog shit for frosting.
speaker: Steve Jobs
context: Critiquing a NeXT programmer's work during a project review, as recounted in Alan Deutschman's biography 'The Second Coming of Steve Jobs' (2000)

text: It's quite valuable to have the courage and the confidence to say, 'No, fuck off, leave me alone, thank you very much.'
speaker: Helen Mirren
context: In a 2013 Daily Mail interview, the Oscar-winning actress said that if she'd had a daughter, "fuck off" is the first phrase she would have taught her, to give her the confidence to set boundaries.

text: I once asked a Jesuit priest what was the best short prayer he knew. He said, "Fuck it," as in "Fuck it; it's in God's hands."
speaker: Anthony Hopkins
context: Anthony Hopkins recounting a Jesuit priest's answer when he asked for the best short prayer — an anecdote he has told publicly, including on Inside the Actors Studio.

text: My secret to a long, happy life? Vodka and hot dogs, probably in that order.
speaker: Betty White
context: The actress and TV legend, asked the secret to her long life, in a 2018 Parade magazine interview at age 95/96.
